title: Self-Compassion Practice Checklist
description: A daily practice guide for building self-compassion. Check off the items you practiced today. Self-compassion is a skill — it strengthens with regular, intentional practice.
instructions: Review each item at the end of your day. Check off what you did, and notice which areas feel natural and which feel challenging. There is no need to check every item — even one or two compassionate actions each day can shift your relationship with yourself over time.

items.label: I noticed when my inner critic was active (without trying to suppress it)
I paused before reacting to a self-critical thought
I named my emotions without judging them (e.g., 'I'm feeling shame right now')
I spoke to myself the way I would speak to a close friend
I used compassionate self-talk when things went wrong (e.g., 'This is hard, and it's okay to struggle')
I practiced a soothing gesture or calming breath when I felt distressed
I reminded myself that struggling is part of being human, not a sign of weakness
I connected with another person (even briefly) rather than withdrawing
I resisted the urge to isolate when feeling low or ashamed
I wrote a self-compassion letter or journal entry
I practiced a loving-kindness or self-compassion meditation
I used the self-compassion break (mindfulness, common humanity, self-kindness)
I did one activity aligned with my values (not to prove my worth, but because it matters to me)
I set a boundary or said no to something that didn't serve me
I acknowledged or celebrated a small win without dismissing it

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes only. Building self-compassion takes time and practice — be patient with yourself. If low self-esteem is significantly affecting your well-being, consider working with a therapist trained in compassion-focused therapy (CFT) or CBT. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
